title: Model SmartCharge NY EV off-peak charging rebate as an add-on to the TOU estimate
description:
public/calc.js's RATES.meta.caveats explicitly documents this gap: 'There is no separate residential EV rate -- EV owners use Residential Time-of-Use (priced here) plus SmartCharge NY rebates (a separate program, not modeled).' EV ownership is a fast-growing segment precisely among the audience already motivated to compare ConEd rate plans (people charging overnight are the textbook off-peak-heavy case TOU rewards), so this caveat is currently the single largest known blind spot in the tool's plan coverage.

Concrete scope: add SmartCharge NY's published per-kWh off-peak charging credit (see coned.com's SmartCharge NY program page for the current rate) as an optional toggle ('I charge an EV at home') in public/index.html; when enabled, public/calc.js applies the credit to off-peak kWh in the TOU cost model (a new costTOU variant or an optional param) and public/app.js surfaces it as an extra line in the 'show the math' breakdown and in the plan table. Keep it opt-in/off by default so the existing Standard-vs-TOU numbers for non-EV households don't change.

title: verify.js doesn't load rates.json -- Node verification can silently diverge from the live site
description:
public/app.js fetches public/rates.json at runtime and overrides calc.js's baked-in RATES defaults (public/calc.js applyRates()) -- this is the actual rate data the live browser tool uses. verify.js (the Node sanity-check script referenced in README.md's 'Verify the math' section) only requires public/calc.js and never reads public/rates.json, so it validates against whatever is hardcoded in calc.js, not what ships to users.

If a future edit updates rates.json without updating calc.js's matching literals (or vice versa), verify.js will report numbers that don't match the live site, defeating its purpose as a trust check. Currently they happen to agree, but nothing enforces that.

Fix: have verify.js JSON.parse public/rates.json and call calc.applyRates(json) before calc.analyze(), same as app.js does. Optionally add a startup assertion that calc.RATES values equal the rates.json values post-merge, so future edits that only touch one file fail loudly instead of drifting silently.

title: Add Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-less) to measure whether coned.jedarden.com is actually used
description:
The site has no analytics of any kind -- there is currently no way to know if anyone visits coned.jedarden.com, or what fraction of visitors get as far as uploading a file vs. bouncing on the landing page. Cloudflare Web Analytics (free, already the hosting platform via CF Pages) is cookie-less and doesn't fingerprint, so it doesn't conflict with the page's existing privacy claim ('nothing is uploaded, stored, or sent anywhere') -- that claim is specifically about the user's uploaded usage data, not standard pageview counting.

Add the CF Web Analytics beacon snippet to public/index.html (or enable it directly in the Cloudflare Pages project settings, no code change needed) and, if it's easy to do without touching user data, a custom event on the 'Try it with a sample home' button click and on successful file parse (public/app.js render()) vs. parse error (showError()) -- this gives a real funnel: visits -> sample-tried -> real-file-uploaded -> error-rate, all without ever transmitting the file contents or the computed result. Note in README.md or the footer that anonymous, cookie-less visit analytics are collected, for transparency.

title: Show an on-page warning when rates.json hasn't been reviewed in 6+ months
description:
public/rates.json's meta.asOf currently reads 'current as of 2026-07' as free-text prose with no machine-checkable date, and public/calc.js's caveats already admit 'Absolute totals are +/-~5%... 2026 months are priced at 2025 rates.' ConEd revises SC1/TOU/demand rates via PSC rate cases roughly annually; nothing here catches the tool quietly going stale.

Add a machine-readable field to rates.json, e.g. "reviewedThrough": "2026-07-01" alongside the existing meta.asOf prose. In public/app.js, compare that date to the current date at render time; if more than ~6 months have elapsed, show a small non-blocking banner ('Rates last verified 2026-07 -- may be out of date; treat as directional') in the results view, similar in style to the existing .legend notes. This makes staleness visible to end users immediately (no external monitoring infra needed) and gives the maintainer a concrete prompt to refresh public/rates.json + bump reviewedThrough on the next pass.

title: Wire push-to-deploy for coned-rate-optimizer via website-build Argo sensor (ADR-001)
description:
Implements docs/plan/plan.md ADR-001. Live site (coned.jedarden.com) currently only gets updated via manual 'wrangler pages deploy' runs -- declarative-config has none of deploy/k8s/coned-bootstrap-configmap.yml, coned-bootstrap-deployment.yml, or coned-deploy-workflow.yml, and iad-ci has zero Argo Workflows ever run for this repo (verified 2026-07-20).

Steps:
1. Copy deploy/k8s/coned-bootstrap-configmap.yml and deploy/k8s/coned-bootstrap-deployment.yml into declarative-config's k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/ directory, commit+push to declarative-config (origin), let ArgoCD sync.
2. Confirm bootstrap pod ran successfully (kubectl logs deploy/coned-bootstrap -n argo-workflows on iad-ci) then remove the bootstrap files per deploy/k8s/README.md Phase 3.
3. Add coned-rate-optimizer to the website-build Argo Events sensor's repo list (same declarative-config path used by the other sites/services in the fleet) so a push to main auto-submits a website-build Workflow.
4. Verify with a trivial no-op commit to this repo that push-to-deploy actually fires and coned.jedarden.com updates.
5. Update DEPLOY.md's 'Direct wrangler' section to say 'break-glass only'.

All cluster/declarative-config changes via commit+push+ArgoCD sync only, never a live kubectl mutation.

title: Add FAQPage JSON-LD structured data to public/index.html for SEO rich snippets
description:
public/index.html already has a well-formed FAQ section (section.faq, 4 Q&A pairs: 'Is ConEd Time-of-Use worth it?', 'What are Steady Use and Smart Energy?', 'Is there a ConEd EV rate?', 'Is my data private?') but no structured data markup. Adding a <script type='application/ld+json'> FAQPage schema (schema.org) referencing the same Q&A text is a small, mechanical change with real upside: Google can render these as expandable rich-result snippets in search for queries like 'is coned time of use worth it', which is likely to meaningfully increase organic click-through for a site with essentially zero paid-acquisition budget.

Keep the JSON-LD text in sync with the visible h3/p content in section.faq (or generate it from the same source at build time if a build step is ever added) so they can't drift apart -- real-world FAQ schema implementations get penalized by Google if they do.
